title: Clone_Spin photo-tile-wall centroid → framing FAR pose
goal: Clone_Spin 10.75 dB → target ≥12.5 (headless oracle 13.41, gap 2.66). DECODED (T-qclonespin1 2026-07-17): Clone_Spin has NO Motion Replicator layer; its 9-tile wall is 9 INDIVIDUAL image DZ tiles named 'Timeline Pin N' (dropZone.type=0, source=transitionA) at world Z=[-1192,-992,-842], X-spread 1489→4737, Y-spread -1592→+1437. Camera has ONE Framing behavior with 'Framing' param id=203 VALUE=4 (unique to Clone_Spin — every other slug uses default 2). The current single-framer WIP dollies near-A → framePose(B), but framePose(B)'s target = B.center + pathOffset in B's oblique local basis pulls target Y up to ~2200 (well ABOVE the tile grid Y-centroid ~-100). Result: all 9 tiles project sy=-1500..-2100 in a 1080 frame (OFF-frame below). Fix: (1) generalize computeWallCenter() in engine/src/evaluator/index.ts to recognize the photo-tile-wall pattern (≥4 image DZ tiles, Z-spread <500, XY-spread >1000 — structural, no slug hardcode), returning the tile bbox centroid; (2) in engine/src/evaluator/framing.ts resolveFramedWallPose single-framer branch (framing.length===1), when wallCenter is defined use it as the FAR camera target (instead of the framer's framePose which points off-wall) — eye stays at framePose(B).pos, target = wallCenter, distance recomputed to fit the wall XY extent into AOV. Guardrails: gate green over Concentric (13.38), Combo_Spin, Video_Wall (2-behavior path — must stay untouched), Duplicate. Expected: Clone_Spin 10.75 → 12-13. Full decode + measured off-scope trials in ROADMAP 2026-07-17q1.
